 {"id":"gt-2sw","title":"Plugin surface for daemon lifecycle hooks","description":"Allow rigs to customize daemon behavior via hooks/plugins.

## Hook Points

- on_heartbeat: Called during daemon heartbeat cycle
- on_worker_idle: Called when worker goes idle
- on_worker_stuck: Called when stuck detection triggers
- on_lifecycle_request: Called before processing lifecycle

## Configuration

```
<rig>/config/daemon-hooks.json
{
  "on_heartbeat": "./hooks/check-ci-status.sh",
  "on_worker_idle": "./hooks/maybe-assign-work.sh",
  "idle_threshold": "5m",
  "custom_signals": [
    {"name": "ci_status", "command": "./hooks/get-ci.sh"}
  ]
}
```

## Signal Contribution

Hooks can return signals that feed into decision engine:
- SKIP_POKE: Don't poke this agent
- FORCE_POKE: Override backoff, poke now
- CUSTOM_DATA: Extra context for logging

## Use Cases

- Check CI status before interrupting test runs
- Auto-assign work when worker becomes idle
- Custom stuck detection for specific workflows","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:34.702624-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:34.702624-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-2sw","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:47.035848-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-5af","title":"Deacon: Hierarchical health-check orchestrator","description":"Replace daemon heartbeats with Deacon - an AI agent that keeps Gas Town running.

## Core Concept

The Deacon is a **Claude agent** (not just a Go process) that:
- Lives at town root in gt-deacon session
- Has its own mailbox (deacon/ identity in town beads)
- Is poked by a minimal Go daemon every ~60s (if idle)
- Monitors Mayor and Witnesses proactively
- Handles lifecycle requests (restart/cycle) from Mayor, Witnesses, AND Crew

## Architecture

```
Minimal Go Daemon (just watches Deacon)
         |
         v
     Deacon (Claude agent)
         |
    +----+----+
    v         v
  Mayor    Witnesses --> Polecats (Witness-managed)
    |         |
    +----+----+
         |
      Crew (lifecycle only, not monitored)
```

## Deacon Responsibilities

**Proactive monitoring:**
- Mayor health (tmux session, keepalive freshness)
- Witness health (tmux sessions, keepalive freshness)

**Reactive lifecycle:**
- Process restart/cycle requests from Mayor, Witnesses, Crew
- Kill session, create new, prime, verify startup

**Escalation:**
- Mail human (configurable) if issues can't be resolved

## Key Files

- ~/gt/DEACON.md - Role context
- ~/gt/deacon/ - State directory
  - heartbeat.json - Written each wake cycle (daemon checks this)
  - state.json - Health tracking, last scan results
- ~/gt/.beads/ - Town beads with deacon/ mail identity

## Wake Cycle

1. Write heartbeat (prevents daemon from poking)
2. Check mail (lifecycle requests)
3. Quick health scan (Mayor, Witnesses)
4. Process lifecycle requests
5. Remediate unhealthy agents
6. Update state

## Session Patterns

- Deacon: gt-deacon
- Mayor: gt-mayor
- Witness: gt-<rig>-witness
- Crew: gt-<rig>-<name> (e.g., gt-gastown-max)

## Relation to Policy Beads

Future: Deacon behavior configurable via policy beads (gt-3zw).
Initial implementation uses sensible defaults.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.083305-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:00:03.948543-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:40:28.64361-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5af","depends_on_id":"gt-3zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:36.617594-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-5wtw","title":"Shutdown request handler","description":"Handle polecat shutdown requests:

When polecat runs 'gt handoff --shutdown':
1. Polecat sends mail to <rig>/witness requesting shutdown
2. Witness receives mail
3. Witness verifies:
   - Git working tree is clean
   - Work is submitted (MR exists or in queue)
   - No uncommitted beads changes
4. If clean:
   - gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
   - git worktree remove polecats/<name>
   - git branch -d polecat/<name>
5. If not clean:
   - Send nudge back to polecat
   - Track retry count

Depends on fixing gt-dsfi (handoff deadlock).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:22.968711-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.300578-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-mxyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:38.893061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-caih","title":"Witness handoff bead state persistence","description":"Implement state persistence for Witness across wisp cycles.

## Problem
Wisps burn between cycles, but Witness needs to remember:
- Which workers have been nudged
- How many times (nudge_count)
- When was last nudge
- Last observed activity

## Solution
Witness handoff bead with worker_states field:

```json
{
  "id": "gt-witness-state",
  "type": "handoff",
  "assignee": "<rig>/witness",
  "pinned": true,
  "worker_states": {
    "furiosa": {
      "issue": "gt-123",
      "nudge_count": 2,
      "last_nudge": "2024-12-22T10:00:00Z"
    }
  },
  "last_patrol": "2024-12-22T10:05:00Z"
}
```

## Implementation
1. On patrol start: bd show <witness-handoff-id> to load state
2. During patrol: update in-memory state
3. On save-state step: bd update to persist
4. State survives wisp burn/squash

 {"id":"gt-caz","title":"Timed Beads: Scheduled recurring work","description":"## Summary

Timed beads wake up periodically and get injected into the ready queue by the daemon.

## Schema Extension

```yaml
id: gt-weekly-sync
type: task  # or sentinel
schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # cron: Monday 9am
# OR
interval: 24h          # every 24 hours
tier: haiku            # cheap model for routine checks
next_run: 2025-12-20T09:00:00Z
```

## Daemon Integration

Daemon heartbeat loop:
1. Check timed beads where `next_run <= now`
2. For each due bead:
   - Inject into ready queue (set status to open if needed)
   - Update `next_run` based on schedule/interval
3. Witnesses pick up work via `bd ready`

## Use Cases

- Weekly team sync reminders
- Daily health checks
- Periodic cleanup tasks
- Scheduled reports

## Interaction with Pinned Beads

A pinned bead can be timed - it wakes up periodically but never closes.
This is how you model "background services" in Gas Town.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-cik","title":"Overseer Crew: User-managed persistent workspaces","description":"## Overview

Crew workers are the overseer's (human's) personal workspaces within a rig. Unlike polecats which are witness-managed and ephemeral, crew workers are:

- **Persistent**: Not auto-garbage-collected
- **User-managed**: Overseer controls lifecycle
- **Long-lived identities**: dave, emma, fred - recognizable names
- **Gas Town integrated**: Mail, handoff mechanics work
- **Tmux optional**: Can work in terminal directly

## Directory Structure

```
<rig>/
  polecats/       # Managed workers (witness controls)
  refinery/       # Merge queue processor
  witness/        # Pit boss
  crew/           # Overseer's personal workspaces
    dave/         # Full clone, persistent
    emma/         # Full clone, persistent
    fred/         # Full clone, persistent
```

## Key Differences from Polecats

- Location: crew/ instead of polecats/
- Lifecycle: User-managed, not witness-managed
- Auto-cleanup: Never (polecats auto-cleanup on swarm land)
- Issue assignment: Optional (polecats require it)
- Tmux: Optional (polecats require it)
- Mail & Handoff: Yes for both
- Identity: Persistent (polecats are ephemeral)

## CLI Commands

- gt crew add <name> [--rig <rig>] - Create crew workspace
- gt crew list [--rig <rig>] - List crew workspaces
- gt crew at <rig>/<name> - Attach to workspace (start session)
- gt crew attach <name> - Attach (infer rig from cwd)
- gt crew refresh <name> - Handoff + restart (context cycling)
- gt crew remove <name> [--force] - Remove workspace
- gt crew status [<name>] - Show workspace status

## Design Notes

- Crew workers use full git clones (not worktrees)
- Optional beads integration via BEADS_DIR
- Mail-to-self handoff works for context cycling
- No witness monitoring or nudging
- No automatic issue assignment required

## Background

Users often maintain separate repo clones for serial agent work. This is tedious to set up manually. Crew workspaces bring these into Gas Town's infrastructure while keeping user control.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:47:37.529887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00"}

## Validation Approaches

### 1. Dry-Run Simulation (Recommended First)
Mayor walks through scenarios mentally/on paper:
- "If polecat Toast signals done with dirty git state, what happens?"
- "If Witness context fills mid-verification, what state is lost?"
- "If two polecats try to close same issue, what happens?"

Create beads for any gaps discovered.

### 2. Real Work in gastown-py
Use Python Gas Town to stress-test assumptions:
- Run actual batch work on test repos
- Observe edge cases in practice
- Document issues found

### 3. Edge Case Analysis
Systematic review of failure modes:
- Agent crashes mid-operation
- Network failures during sync
- Concurrent access to shared state
- Context limits hit at bad times

## Key Scenarios to Validate

- [ ] Witness session cycling (state preservation)
- [ ] Polecat decommission with dirty state
- [ ] Merge conflicts in queue
- [ ] Beads sync conflicts between workers
- [ ] Escalation path (stuck worker -> Mayor)
- [ ] Cross-rig communication
- [ ] Federation mail routing (future)

## Success Criteria

- No data loss scenarios identified
- Clear recovery paths for all failure modes
- Edge cases either handled or documented as limitations
- Design improves as model cognition improves

## Output

1. Document in relevant bead if issue found
2. Create new beads for missing functionality
3. Update architecture.md if design changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:24:11.251841-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:49.858717-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-nti8","title":"Polecats should not push branches to remote","description":"## Current Behavior

Polecats push their branches to origin (e.g., `polecat/furiosa`), which pollutes the remote with many short-lived branches.

## Desired Behavior

Polecats should only commit locally. The Refinery handles all remote pushes:
1. Polecat works on local `polecat/<name>` branch
2. Polecat signals done (state → idle)
3. Refinery pulls from local polecat branch
4. Refinery runs tests, merges to main
5. Refinery pushes main to remote
6. If PR review needed, Refinery creates the PR

## Benefits

- Clean remote (no branch pollution)
- Clear responsibility (Refinery is the quality gate)
- Simpler cleanup (local branches deleted with worktree)
- Less noise in GitHub UI

## Trade-offs

- If polecat crashes before Refinery merges, code is lost locally
- But beads issue remains open, another polecat can redo the work
- This is acceptable for ephemeral workers

## Implementation

In polecat CLAUDE.md or landing protocol:
- Remove `git push origin HEAD` from workflow
- Replace with just `git commit` + signal done
- Refinery handles the rest","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00"}

A plugin at `<rig>/plugins/plan-oracle/` that:
- Receives planning requests via mail

- Creates structured beads epic
- Sets dependencies based on analysis

```bash
gt plan create
# Walks through questions, creates epic interactively
```

As stated: 'The Planning phase should end in the creation of a workable Beads plan.'

This plugin bridges the gap between human planning and machine-executable work.

 {"id":"gt-zut3","title":"Immediate daemon notification on lifecycle request","description":"When gt handoff sends a lifecycle request to the daemon (via bd mail send deacon/), the daemon only discovers it on its next heartbeat poll (every 5 min). Workers wait unnecessarily for retirement.

## Current Behavior
1. Worker runs gt handoff
2. Lifecycle mail sent to deacon/ via beads
3. Worker blocks waiting for retirement
4. Daemon heartbeat runs (up to 5 min later)
5. Daemon processes lifecycle request and kills session

## Expected Behavior
Lifecycle requests should be processed immediately, not on poll interval.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A: SIGUSR1 Signal (simplest)
1. Add SIGUSR1 handler in daemon that calls ProcessLifecycleRequests()
2. gt handoff reads daemon.pid and sends SIGUSR1 after mail send

### Option B: Unix Socket
1. Daemon listens on ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.sock
2. gt handoff connects and sends 'process' message

### Option C: Watchfile + fsnotify
1. gt handoff touches ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.trigger
2. Daemon uses fsnotify to detect and process

Recommend Option A for simplicity.

## Affected Files
- internal/daemon/daemon.go - add signal handler
- internal/cmd/handoff.go - send signal after mail","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:20:07.616335-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:27:42.419089-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:27:42.419089-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-zx3","title":"Per-rig beads repo configuration","description":"Add per-rig beads configuration to rig config schema.

## Config Schema

In each rig's config.json:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "name": "wyvern",
  "git_url": "https://github.com/steveyegge/wyvern",
  "beads": {
    "repo": "local",    // "local" | "<path>" | "<git-url>"
    "root": null,       // Override bd --root (optional)
    "prefix": "wyv"     // Issue prefix for this rig
  }
}
```

## Repo Options

| Value | Meaning | Use Case |
|-------|---------|----------|
| `"local"` | Use project's `.beads/` | Own projects, full commit access |
| `"<path>"` | Use beads at path | OSS contributions |
| `"<git-url>"` | Clone and use repo | Team shared beads |

## Environment Injection

When spawning polecats, Gas Town sets:
```bash
export BEADS_ROOT="<resolved-path>"
```

## Resolution Logic

```go
func ResolveBeadsRoot(rigConfig *RigConfig, rigPath string) (string, error) {
    beads := rigConfig.Beads
    switch {
    case beads.Root != "":
        return beads.Root, nil
    case beads.Repo == "local" || beads.Repo == "":
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, ".beads"), nil
    case strings.HasPrefix(beads.Repo, "/"):
        return beads.Repo, nil
    case strings.Contains(beads.Repo, "://"):
        return cloneAndResolve(beads.Repo)
    default:
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, beads.Repo), nil
    }
}
```

## Backwards Compatibility

If `beads` section missing, assume `"repo": "local"`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:16.660049-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:02.122203-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zx3","depends_on_id":"gt-l3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:35.726502-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
